action: sub-leading Birkhoff [n^-1] balance, c1 coefficient and resonance test
key_finding: After reducing modulo the characteristic equation mu^2 = b1*mu - a2 and substituting the W1 indicial closed form for alpha, the c1-coefficient at [u^1] is A_num = (a2 - b1*mu)*(4*a2 - b1^2). It vanishes only on (i) the degenerate locus mu = a2/b1 (= mu = 0 by char eq) and (ii) the double-root locus 4*a2 = b1^2 (CF-Alg discriminant zero), neither of which is the Trans locus a2 = -2*b1^2/9. On Trans: A | mu_+- = -+sqrt(17)*b1/3 (non-zero). c1 is uniquely determined by [u^1] balance (c1 = -B/A); the sub-leading order yields no new constraint distinguishing Trans.

action: convergence rate r_n = p_n/q_n - L for 10 Trans + 5 Log + 5 Alg families, mp.dps=200, N=600
key_finding: For Trans (and Log) families log10|r_n - L| reaches -200 by n~300, i.e. it falls below mp.dps. The classical normalization rho_n = |r_n - L| * q_n^2 explodes (10^2620 at n=500) because q_n grows hyperexponentially as Gamma(n+1)*mu^n for degree-(2,1) recurrences, not as a Diophantine polynomial. The natural rate constant is |mu_-/mu_+|, which on the Trans locus is the Vieta-tautological 4/(13+3*sqrt(17)) already established in W2. No new amplitude/coefficient invariant is exposed at this precision.

action: direct PSLQ on CF limits L for 10 Trans families, mp.dps=300
key_finding: C1: PSLQ on [1, L, L^2, L^3, L^4] with maxcoeff=10000 found NO relation for any of the 10 Trans L values. C2: pairwise PSLQ on [1, L_i, L_j, L_i*L_j, L_i^2, L_j^2] returned 10 relations, all of which are tautological consequences of the b1 -> lambda*b1 scale invariance for the (a0,a1,b0)=(0,0,0) sub-family, e.g. L(b1=6) = 2*L(b1=3), L(b1=9) = 3*L(b1=3); these are not phantom by the L-coeff rule but are recognised as tautologies by post-hoc inspection. C3: PSLQ on [1, L, sqrt(17), pi, log(2)] found NO relation. Conclusion: the Trans CF limits L are not algebraic to bound 10000 and admit no obvious closed form against the standard transcendental basis.

All three Birkhoff-style analytic frameworks have now been shown insufficient to characterise the Trans locus a2/b1^2 = -2/9. W1 ruled out the strong-form indicial conjecture. W2 showed the |mu_+/mu_-| identity (13+3*sqrt(17))/4 is Vieta-tautological. W3 shows: (A) the [n^-1] sub-leading balance is regular on Trans and yields no new constraint; (B) the convergence rate constant is |mu_-/mu_+|, again W2-tautological; (C) the limits L are not algebraic to bound 10000 nor expressible against {sqrt(17), pi, log 2}. Recommendation: write an EMPIRICAL paper based on the existing 585k-family Trans/Log/Alg classification + W1's indicial closed form + W2's PSLQ identity. The Trans property a2/b1^2 = -2/9 should be reported as an empirically robust but currently analytically opaque invariant.
